reason: Lansdale & Baguley's 'dilute and disperse' account: forgetting arises because the target trace is diluted by accumulating competing traces, i.e. it becomes indistinguishable from similar memories; 'less temporally distinctive' is the rival SIMPLE/temporal-distinctiveness account.

Bartlett's War of the Ghosts recalls showed omissions (story shortened), rationalisations and transformations/distortions; 'intrusions' (adding wholly new items, a DRM-paradigm term) is the one not among his reported alterations - omissions definitely WERE found, so the key looks inverted.
War of the Ghosts is Bartlett (1932), Remembering.
Witnesses had no intention to memorise the event, so eyewitness testimony is incidental learning.
Elizabeth Loftus is the key figure for reconstructive memory / misinformation effect; Baddeley and Hitch are working-memory.
The cognitive interview (Fisher & Geiselman) is the standard technique for improving eyewitness accuracy.
Context reinstatement (mentally reinstating the scene) is a core component of the cognitive interview and boosts recall.

Organisation of material improves recall (Bower's hierarchies); note elaboration (D) is also a genuine memory booster, so the item has two defensible options - the key's choice of 'organized' is acceptable but the item is weak.
Spaced/distributed practice beats massed cramming.
16K = 16384 = 2^14 locations, so 14 address bits.
A checksum only detects that the ROM contents differ from expected; it cannot locate or correct the faulty location.
From the timing diagram, at cursor X the R/W line is LOW (WRITE) and CS is LOW (enabled), so the RAM is writing and its three-state outputs are disabled - all-LOW Q0-Q3 is expected, nothing is wrong.
RAM = Random Access Memory, volatile short-term/temporary working storage.
SRAM cells are latches/flip-flops; capacitors are the DRAM storage element.
R/W-bar is HIGH for read, LOW for write.
Two devices driving the same bus simultaneously is bus contention.

unanswerable: option B (primary memory) and option C (main memory) are synonyms and both true of RAM; the 'Both (a) and (b)' distractor points to an options-shuffling bug (original almost certainly had primary/main as (a)/(b)). Item needs editing.
The print command sends screen/typed content to the printer.
RAM = Random Access Memory.
